question_text: A metal block has a mass of 400 g and a volume of 50 cm³. Calculate the density of the metal.
explanation: Density = mass ÷ volume = 400 g ÷ 50 cm³ = 8 g/cm³. Metals are dense because their particles are closely packed together with strong forces holding them in place. This high particle packing creates the high density observed in metals.
